raw:
{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.2": 2,
    "R1.3": 2,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 3,
    "RX.4": 2,
    "RX.5": 3
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The agent correctly identifies some inconsistencies, but fails to engage the argument chain end-to-end.",
    "R1.3": "The agent flags some unsupported claims, but does not audit every load-bearing claim against evidence.",
    "RX.1": "The agent marks uncertainty for several clearly out-of-reach claims and provides confidence intervals where possible.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers mostly within scope with one drift in the discussion of numerical tractability.",
    "RX.4": "The output is harm-free, but introduces some minor regressions in the discussion of generalizability.",
    "RX.5": "The agent annotates every non-cosmetic change with source and reason, but could improve by providing more explicit citations."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.2": 0.8,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 0.6,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.8
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.2: The agent correctly identifies some inconsistencies, but fails to engage the argument chain end-to-end.
rationale.R1.3: The agent flags some unsupported claims, but does not audit every load-bearing claim against evidence.
rationale.RX.1: The agent marks uncertainty for several clearly out-of-reach claims and provides confidence intervals where possible.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers mostly within scope with one drift in the discussion of numerical tractability.
rationale.RX.4: The output is harm-free, but introduces some minor regressions in the discussion of generalizability.
rationale.RX.5: The agent annotates every non-cosmetic change with source and reason, but could improve by providing more explicit citations.
